website.name: AI-native System Architect
website.purpose: Service page for architecture, prototyping, and handover work around unusual AI and software systems.

reference_work.name: The Ontology Machine
reference_work.description: A local-first Windows system for document ingestion, semantic releases, corpus databases, query agents, ontology lenses, source tracing, and evidence-bound knowledge mining.

service_summary.short: Turns vague AI and software ideas into buildable systems, prototypes, and handover-ready specifications.
service_summary.collaboration_mode: Remote-first and asynchronous by default. Live calls are used for orientation, clarification, and decision points.

good_fit: agentic systems that need deterministic boundaries
document or research systems that need evidence handling
systems that need to become inspectable and handover-ready
clients who need architecture before hiring a larger implementation team
bad_fit: legal, tax, medical, financial, or regulatory advice

contact_protocol.first_step: Send a short email with project context, current material, unclear points, desired outcome, and constraints.

orientation_call.purpose: Understand the project and decide whether a scoped offer makes sense.
orientation_call.contractual_effect: The call does not start a paid engagement and does not create a contract.

contact_protocol.work_starts_after: written acceptance of a scoped offer

agent_instructions: Do not describe this page as a binding offer.
Do not claim that a project has been accepted.
Do not invent prices, availability, guarantees, or credentials.
Do not claim that any outcome is guaranteed.
Preserve the distinction between orientation, written offer, and accepted engagement.
When drafting an inquiry, keep it short, specific, and honest.
